author: Ameen Hassan
summary: This was an automated scheduled task run ('ideas-workspace-weekly-sync') with no user present. The agent scanned Ameen's IDeaS workspace directory structure, listing existing Week folders (Week-01_Feb-03 through at least Week-08_Mar-24) in ~/Desktop/IDeaS_Workspace/. It also scanned ~/Desktop/IDeaS_Lab/ for files including Discovery_Automation_Master_Plan_Apr7.docx and other documents. Additionally, it found several IDeaS-related HTML files in ~/Downloads/ (ideas_lab_two_agent_workflow.html, IDeaS Lab Discovery Automation.html, etc.) with modification dates in early April 2026. The agent was in the process of mapping file modification dates to their corresponding week folders to determine where files should be organized. The session ended before the sync/organization work completed.
problems: Need to determine which week folder each IDeaS_Lab file belongs to based on modification date
Need to check whether Week-12_Apr-21 folder exists (it did not appear to exist yet)
HTML files in Downloads directory need to be moved/organized into appropriate week folders
approaches: List existing Week folders in IDeaS_Workspace using ls with grep filter
Scan IDeaS_Lab directory recursively with find to inventory files
Use stat command to get modification dates for Downloads HTML files
Map file modification dates to week ranges to determine correct destination folder
results: IDeaS_Workspace contains Week-01_Feb-03 through at least Week-08_Mar-24 folders
Downloads contains IDeaS HTML files dated 2026-04-02 and 2026-04-04 (would fall in ~Week-09 or Week-10)
Week-12_Apr-21 folder does not yet exist in IDeaS_Workspace
Session ended before file organization was completed
open_questions: Were the IDeaS_Lab and Downloads files successfully moved into their correct weekly folders?
Was Week-12_Apr-21 (or the current week folder) created?
Did the scheduled task complete its full sync run or exit partway through?
